239914.explanation: The decision is already made, and reversing it would lead to millions in losses. Something had to happen to the town hall anyway. We find new construction too expensive. Renovating the town hall is cheaper.

239917.explanation: We see the trend of Citizen Assemblies emerging. Leefbaar Zeewolde is sympathetic to this instrument. We are willing to conduct a pilot and closely follow developments.

239918.explanation: Given developments from The Hague where less money goes to the municipality, we cannot reduce the property tax (OZB). We can strive to prevent a steep increase. The municipality needs this income to continue financing all village facilities.

239919.explanation: Leefbaar Zeewolde aims for a society where everyone can participate, including people with limited financial means. Furthermore, sports and culture promote physical and mental health.

239921.explanation: The forest remains just a forest but will be made more accessible and attractive to better enjoy. The alternative is building homes. Moreover, the decision is already made, and reversing it would waste public funds.

239922.explanation: The municipality should not burden businesses with additional environmental regulations; that is not the municipality's role. The municipality should enforce national environmental rules.

239923.explanation: This is already being done by, among others, Puur Zeewolde. Leefbaar Zeewolde wholeheartedly supports this.

239924.explanation: Broad prosperity and a steadily growing economy go hand in hand according to Leefbaar Zeewolde. Organic growth from entrepreneurs is central. Suitable employment reduces commuting in and out.

239925.explanation: There must of course be guidelines for opening and closing times, with differences between hospitality and retail.

239928.explanation: Leefbaar Zeewolde aims for a society where everyone can participate, including our youth. Cutting youth care would lead to growing waiting lists. Especially during that phase of their lives, they must receive help if needed.

239929.explanation: Leefbaar Zeewolde supports housing market circulation but not prioritizing status holders.

239930.explanation: Good integration promotes newcomers' well-being, which also benefits Zeewolde. The Zeewolde municipality has sufficient financial resources for this.

239931.explanation: It is primarily the responsibility of companies to provide housing for migrant workers they employ. If a company falls short, the municipality must address this.

239932.explanation: The municipality must state that the fire station should be as energy self-sufficient as possible, preferably 100%. This prevents further overloading our power grid, so residents and entrepreneurs are not left out due to network congestion.

239933.explanation: Not to ban, but we follow provincial and government policies for cleaner air (Clean Air Agreement). The municipality can emphasize that open fireplaces and wood stoves emit a lot of fine particles, so it is better not to use them or only with good filters.

239934.explanation: To keep waste costs affordable, we must produce as little residual waste as possible. If the municipality wants residents to act more sustainably, it must provide the means to do so.

239935.explanation: The forest edges form the natural framing of Zeewolde and greatly contribute to its village character, unique in the Netherlands. Building homes in these areas undermines Zeewolde’s identity. It is good to formalize this by designating them as a nature reserve.

239936.explanation: Leefbaar was against the arrival of the large solar park in the forest. The business model for solar panels has completely changed in recent years; therefore, Leefbaar Zeewolde is now against more solar parks in meadows.

239937.explanation: Given the nitrogen problem, we must ensure that our farmers with livestock can maintain their businesses.

239938.explanation: It is not the municipality’s task to provide urban agriculture. Leefbaar Zeewolde does want to support initiatives from society or entrepreneurs, as has happened in the past with the MMM farm and various food forests.

239940.explanation: Leefbaar Zeewolde aims for a society where everyone can participate, including people with limited financial means. These funds are already available in Zeewolde municipality.

239941.explanation: Art and culture must be accessible to everyone in Zeewolde. Cutting budgets would mean they become available only to the elite. Leefbaar Zeewolde finds that undesirable.

239942.explanation: The associations themselves indicate they do not want to move to a new sports complex. It would be capital destruction of current facilities. There is enough space at the current sports complex, also for the ambitions of the associations and their expected future growth.

239943.explanation: We believe that young people from Zeewolde should be able to follow their studies close to home.

239944.explanation: Artists from Zeewolde should be given priority. This stimulates art in general within our own village.

239946.explanation: BOAs (official enforcement officers) must be able to protect themselves in dangerous situations. Of course, this decision will always be made in consultation with the BOAs. BOAs should never replace the police. The condition is that BOAs receive regular good training in the use of batons.

239948.explanation: The experiments and establishment of legal cannabis cultivation are still ongoing. Whether it leads to a reduction in crime, and whether it causes nuisance, is still unclear. Therefore, a coffeeshop is not a good idea for the time being.

239949.explanation: In our view, the police should have to rely as little as possible on camera footage from businesses and private individuals during investigations. Entrepreneurs and private citizens indicate that they do not feel completely safe in this regard.

239950.explanation: The current situation, where young people in Zeewolde find it very difficult to get a social rental home in Zeewolde, is actually unacceptable for Leefbaar Zeewolde. Within the local scope available, housing seekers from Zeewolde must be given priority when allocating social rental housing.

239951.explanation: A diverse housing supply is essential for livability. The policy must be clear, transparent, and focused on local needs and new residents. The successful formula of one-third social and affordable – one-third mid-range – one-third expensive remains the principle.

239952.explanation: High-rise buildings do not fit the village character of Zeewolde. Leefbaar Zeewolde sees more opportunities for more compact houses and apartments to provide a better affordable housing supply and improved housing flow.

239953.explanation: According to Leefbaar Zeewolde, Oosterwold can perfectly function as a second core within the municipal area of Zeewolde. This can provide room for 10,000–15,000 houses.

239954.explanation: Without rules, there is too great a risk of uncontrolled building or renovating without involving the surrounding environment.

239955.explanation: Leefbaar Zeewolde was and remains against permanent residence on recreational parks where this is not allowed according to the zoning plan. Aside from the fact that every buyer knew what was and wasn’t allowed, permitting permanent residence has enormous financial consequences for the municipality.

239956.explanation: There is a housing shortage, so building must take place. In our view, not in the forests. When purchasing agricultural land, the interests and input of the farmers must be taken into account. Oosterwold and A-area only, no further expansion! We are building a village, not a city.

239957.explanation: The forest edges form the natural framing of Zeewolde and strongly contribute to the village character, which is unique in the Netherlands. Housing in these areas undermines Zeewolde’s identity. The forest will be made more accessible and attractive to better enjoy it.

239959.explanation: The center must become more lively and attractive. That includes the Aanloophaven.

239960.explanation: Everything that was once built on the dike body can never be rebuilt after demolition, neither for residential nor work purposes. Therefore, no apartments can be built on the municipal yard.

239961.explanation: The poverty policy "Do what is necessary" has already been initiated by the municipality of Zeewolde. This also includes easing the regulations.

239962.explanation: The focus must be on guiding them (back) to the labor process. But where possible, they can do socially meaningful work.

239963.explanation: Subsidized jobs help bridge the gap to the labor market and can lead to more sustainable employment for people distant from the labor market. However, certain conditions must be met, such as good supervision.

239965.explanation: Leefbaar Zeewolde finds this a very strange plan. What problem does it solve? And for whom? Zeewolde is well accessible and has free and sufficient parking options.

239966.explanation: Leefbaar Zeewolde finds a fixed shore connection between Zeewolde and Ermelo an illogical choice, considering the costs and the impact on peace and nature.

239967.explanation: At this moment, there is no problem that the introduction of a low emission zone would solve. It would cause problems, making Zeewolde less accessible.

239969.explanation: Holiday flights will cause a lot of nuisance in Zeewolde. The municipality must use all instruments and possibilities it has to prevent this.

239970.explanation: Flights will cause a lot of nuisance in Zeewolde. The municipality must deploy all instruments and possibilities it has to achieve this.

239971.explanation: Leefbaar Zeewolde aims for a society in which everyone can participate, including our youth. Precisely in that period of their lives, they should receive help if needed. Moreover, youth and young adult work is preventive and thus saves higher costs in the long term.

239972.explanation: Low-threshold youth care is preventive and prevents problems and higher costs. Especially in a time of increasing crime and mental challenges among youth, youth assistance is of utmost importance.

239973.explanation: Good information in schools and neighborhood centers, along with appropriate supplies such as sanitary pads and tampons, is important.

239974.explanation: This should be seen as a joint responsibility of the schools together with the municipality.

239975.explanation: If this situation arises, the municipality of Zeewolde must provide shelter locations.

239914.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde stands for reliable governance and careful management of public money. The renovation is not a luxury but a necessity. The working conditions of the current town hall do not meet the labor regulations.

239917.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde is positive towards citizen participation. A citizens’ assembly can be a suitable instrument under certain conditions.

239918.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde wants to keep municipal charges for residents as low as possible. If the financial situation allows, lowering the property tax (ozb) is a logical and desirable choice.

239919.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde finds it important that everyone can participate, even when money is tight. Sports and culture contribute to health and social interaction. A discount pass helps remove barriers for people with low income.

239921.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde stands for reliable governance. The sitting municipal council approved the Ontmoetingsbos (Meeting Forest), the process is ongoing, and reversing it would be irresponsible. Additionally, we oppose cutting down trees for housing and choose for green preservation.

239922.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde believes that companies establishing here must take responsibility for people and the environment. Strict environmental standards protect nature, health, and ensure a sustainable future for the village.

239923.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde sees opportunities in recreation and liveliness but is cautious with expensive promotional campaigns. Initiatives should fit the village character and mainly arise from local entrepreneurs and events.

239924.explanation: There is currently enough space for companies to establish themselves. Extra space would come at the expense of valuable agricultural land and space for housing development.

239925.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde gives entrepreneurs room but also believes that opening hours must be balanced with livability, tranquility, and community life. Complete freedom without frameworks does not fit that.

239928.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde believes youth assistance must always be available for children and families who need it. Budget cuts to youth care directly affect vulnerable youth and do not align with caring and people-oriented governance.

239929.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde believes everyone has the right to a fair chance at housing. At the same time, the housing shortage is significant. The municipality is legally obliged to house a certain number of status holders. We must comply with that.

239930.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde finds it important that newcomers can participate well in society. Investing in guidance, language, and social interaction helps integration, prevents problems, and strengthens the social cohesion in the village.

239931.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde believes that labor migrants should not end up homeless. If people lose their housing, the municipality must help find alternative accommodation, out of human dignity and responsibility.

239932.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde believes that the arrival of the barracks must not lead to additional pressure on the energy network. Therefore, it is important that the national government commits to a barracks that largely supplies its own energy.

239933.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde values a healthy living environment but also respects the freedom of residents. We want to make such decisions only in consultation with the residents.

239934.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde wants to maximize waste reuse. A circular center with a recycling station and repair workshop helps to extend the life of items, reduce waste, and actively involve people in sustainability.

239935.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde has agreed during this period to the further development of the forest in the G-area. The process is already underway. Reversing it does not fit with reliable governance and our choice for green and nature development.

239936.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde wants to promote sustainable energy but is also careful with the landscape and agricultural land. Solar panels in meadows therefore require careful consideration and are not an automatic choice.

239937.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde wants to welcome entrepreneurs who want to invest in the future. If a new barn is built sustainably, innovatively, and circularly, and animal welfare is well safeguarded, this should be possible in the rural area.

239938.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde sees urban agriculture as a great way to combine living, food production, and social interaction. It contributes to sustainability, involvement, and a green living environment.

239940.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde believes every child should be able to play sports, regardless of their parents’ income. Sports promote health, self-confidence, and participation. Money should never be a barrier.

239941.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde believes that budget cuts should not automatically affect arts and culture. These contribute to social interaction, wellbeing, and livability. Cutting budgets requires a broad and careful consideration.

239942.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde considers sports important and wants to keep sports accessible to everyone. A new sports complex may be an option but is not an explicit goal. Its usefulness, costs, location, and impact on the surroundings must be carefully weighed.

239943.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde sees opportunities in the arrival of the barracks. By urging the Defense Ministry to offer vocational and higher education on site, we can strengthen education, employment, and future prospects for young people.

239944.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde values giving local artists a place. Art from our own village strengthens recognition, involvement, and pride, and contributes to social encounters in public spaces.

239946.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde values safety but also de-escalation and carefulness.

239948.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde believes that a coffeeshop does not fit with a safe and healthy village. Addictions do not belong here. We want to focus on prevention, health, and a livable environment for all residents.

239949.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde considers safety important but does not see camera surveillance as the only solution. It can be useful in specific places but always combined with prevention, supervision, and careful consideration of privacy.

239950.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde thinks it is important that residents of Zeewolde maintain prospects for affordable housing in their own village. When housing is scarce, it makes sense to consider this in allocation, within fair and legal frameworks.

239951.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde considers sufficient social rental housing important but does not want to impose a fixed percentage. The right distribution depends on location, target group, and capacity. Tailored solutions are needed to keep neighborhoods livable and mixed.

239952.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde is cautious about high-rise buildings. In some situations, building up to six floors can be appropriate, but only if done carefully and fitting the village character, surroundings, and livability.

239953.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde sees Oosterwold as a logical place for further growth. A second residential core can gradually arise there, with room for housing, urban agriculture, and local initiative, fitting the Oosterwold character.

239954.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde supports the current policy, which gives residents freedom to build and renovate in a way that suits them, as long as quality, safety, and livability are well secured.

239955.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde recognizes the housing shortage but sees permanent habitation of recreational homes as not a good solution. Recreation areas are not intended for this and it can lead to problems with livability, safety, and facilities.

239956.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde is careful with agricultural land but acknowledges limited space. If no realistic alternatives exist, new development on agricultural land may be necessary, provided it is done carefully with attention to landscape and the future.

239957.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde has agreed to the Meeting Forest and the process has already begun. Reversing this to build homes does not fit with reliable governance. We consciously choose to preserve green space and oppose cutting down trees.

239959.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde sees the Aanloophaven as a place with great potential for social interaction and recreation. Further development with appropriate hospitality can contribute to vibrancy, provided it remains small-scale and fits the surroundings.

239960.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde sees opportunities for housing in suitable locations, but relocating the municipal yard and building apartments requires careful consideration. Location, costs, nuisance, and added value for the surroundings are decisive.

239961.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde believes that assistance with poverty must be accessible and humane. Arrangements should be simpler and more flexible so that people who need support dare to and can actually use it.

239962.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde believes that people on welfare should not be obliged to work as a condition for benefits. We choose trust and a human approach. However, we encourage voluntary work if it suits someone’s situation.

239963.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde believes everyone should be able to participate. Subsidized jobs help people who face barriers to the labor market gain work experience, structure, and regain perspective.

239965.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde recognizes the importance of good accessibility and sustainable transport. Park & Ride with rental bikes and charging stations can help reduce traffic in the village but requires careful consideration of costs, location, and usage.

239966.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde considers a better connection with Harderwijk and Ermelo important. Therefore, the municipality should keep urging the provinces for a tunnel or bridge to improve accessibility, safety, and cycling options.

239967.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde believes a low emission zone is unnecessary because Zeewolde does not have major problems with issues like air pollution. Such a measure does not fit the scale and village character and unnecessarily restricts residents and businesses.

239969.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde believes holiday flights from Lelystad Airport do not fit with the peace, health, and livability of Zeewolde. The municipality should actively continue to oppose this. We focus on reducing the number of flights from a sustainability perspective.

239970.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde prioritizes the peace and health of residents. If Lelystad Airport still opens, the municipality must strive for a night flight ban to prevent nuisance and disturbance of the living environment.

239971.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde values youth and young people’s work for prevention, social interaction, and perspective. Additional investment helps prevent problems and supports young people in growing up and participating.

239972.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde believes youth care must be accessible and low-threshold. Stricter rules make it harder for children and families to get timely help and can worsen problems.

239973.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde believes that period poverty must be addressed. The best way to do this should be further researched.

239974.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde considers prevention of addiction among young people important. Since Zeewolde has only one small secondary school and many young people attend school in Ermelo and Harderwijk, cooperation with those municipalities is logical and effective.

239975.explanation: ChristenUnie Zeewolde believes that no one should end up on the street. Additional shelter places for homeless and displaced people are needed to provide safety, calm, and guidance and to prevent further problems.

239914.explanation: The VVD is critical of the current renovation plans. The reserved €10 million is disproportionate for merely making workplaces compliant with occupational health and safety standards. We advocate a sober consideration between renovation and new construction, ensuring every tax euro is spent efficiently.

239917.explanation: The VVD believes in the power of representative democracy. Residents elect their municipal council to make decisions. We prefer participation where expertise and involvement are central over lottery systems, in order to safeguard democratic legitimacy.

239918.explanation: The VVD wants to keep the burdens for residents and entrepreneurs as low as possible. A lower property tax (OZB) stimulates homeownership and leaves people with more money in their pockets. We aim for an efficient government so that tax reductions remain possible.

239919.explanation: Income policy is a task for the national government, not the municipality. The VVD believes that existing national schemes are sufficient. We prefer to focus on encouraging work and self-reliance, rather than creating extra local bureaucratic passes and schemes. We also want to make sports accessible for the lowest incomes, which requires tailored solutions.

239921.explanation: Blocking homes in the G-area is detrimental to house hunters. By reversing this decision, we save millions in construction costs and generate income (€24 million) for the community through land sales.

239922.explanation: The VVD wants an attractive business climate for entrepreneurs. We comply with national environmental laws and do not want extra local "heads" on policies that unnecessarily complicate entrepreneurship. We prefer to stimulate innovation and sustainability through cooperation rather than additional prohibitions.

239923.explanation: Tourism and recreation are important drivers for our local economy. By actively putting Zeewolde on the map as an attractive destination, we stimulate entrepreneurs’ turnover, increase employment and strengthen the liveliness in our village and surrounding area.

239924.explanation: There must be sufficient business park space available to meet demand. More opportunities for working within the municipality are good for the environment and our residents.

239925.explanation: Entrepreneurial freedom is central to the VVD. We trust that entrepreneurs themselves are best positioned to assess when they want to serve their customers. The government should facilitate rather than restrict this, including broader possibilities for Sunday openings.

239928.explanation: We want care to always be available for those who truly need it. By organizing youth care more efficiently, smarter, and closer to the resident, we can keep control over costs without compromising quality. Cutting bureaucracy is necessary to keep care affordable.

239929.explanation: The VVD stands for equal opportunities for everyone in the housing market. Priority for specific groups undermines the sense of fairness among other house hunters, such as starters and families who have been waiting a long time. We focus on building more for everyone instead of unequal distribution.

239930.explanation: Integration is primarily a personal responsibility. Sufficient (national) programs are already available. The fastest route to integration is participation through work, volunteering and neighborhood contact, rather than solely extra municipal subsidies.

239931.explanation: The responsibility for decent housing for labor migrants lies primarily with the employer and the migrant themselves. The municipality has a supervisory role on quality and safety but does not act as a general rental office for specific target groups outside the regular waiting lists.

239932.explanation: Although sustainability is important, it must be technically and economically feasible. Given current grid congestion and national defense interests, the national government must retain room for a realistic energy supply that does not jeopardize the operational readiness of the barracks.

239933.explanation: The VVD stands for individual freedom and property rights. We oppose a ban on wood burning, but support good education about responsible burning to minimize nuisance for the environment.

239934.explanation: The VVD believes that the recycling center should primarily focus on efficient waste separation and processing. For repairs and reuse we prefer space for private initiatives and market entrepreneurs rather than a municipal-facilitated workshop.

239935.explanation: Housing construction is the VVD’s top priority to address the housing shortage. The G-area, due to its location near existing infrastructure, is the most logical place to quickly and affordably build new homes for starters and families.

239936.explanation: We choose 'solar panels on roofs' and parking lots. Agricultural land is scarce and essential for our food production and open landscape. Moreover, this choice prevents extra strain on the electricity grid at locations where there is no demand.

239937.explanation: The VVD opposes generic bans that hinder entrepreneurship. Innovation in the agricultural sector must remain possible. Provided the strictest environmental standards are met and integration into the landscape is guaranteed, scaling up or modernization should be allowed.

239938.explanation: Food production is a craft that requires expertise and space. The VVD prefers strengthening our professional agricultural sector in the outskirts rather than small-scale, municipally subsidized farming projects within the core.

239940.explanation: Every child deserves a fair start and the chance to participate. The VVD supports the Youth Fund for Sports & Culture because sports are not only healthy but also contribute to the social development and equal opportunities of children.

239941.explanation: When necessary to cut spending, the VVD chooses to protect core tasks such as safety, care, and infrastructure. Subsidies for arts and culture are not sacred cows for us; we expect greater financial independence from organizations.

239942.explanation: We choose to optimally utilize and modernize the current centrally located sports facilities. This keeps sports accessible for everyone, close to schools, and saves high costs for new infrastructure on the village’s edge.

239943.explanation: The arrival of the barracks offers unique opportunities for Zeewolde. We want the barracks not to become a closed fortress but connected to our society. Requests for educational facilities (MBO/HBO) on the site can strengthen the local labor market, but Defense manages its own needs.

239944.explanation: The VVD values local entrepreneurs and talent, but quality and the best price-quality ratio come first. We encourage using local artists when they fit within Zeewolde’s quality ambitions, without excluding other professional creators by default.

239946.explanation: If we ask our enforcement officers to act in difficult situations, they must have the means to protect themselves and others. The VVD supports nationally and locally equipping BOAs with necessary defensive tools, provided they have received the proper training.

239948.explanation: VVD Zeewolde chooses a safe and family-friendly village. The arrival of a coffeeshop entails risks of nuisance and crime and does not suit the character of our municipality. We prefer to focus on prevention and enforcement against illegal trade.

239949.explanation: Camera surveillance can be an effective tool for both prevention and investigation. Where safety is at risk or where there is structural nuisance, the municipality must be able to deploy this tool proportionally. We want the municipality to take the lead in a safe Zeewolde.

239950.explanation: We prefer to build primarily for our own residents. It is unacceptable that Zeewolde residents have to wait for years while they are rooted here. The VVD wants to use the maximum legal space to give priority to local residents in the housing regulations.

239951.explanation: A fixed 45% social rental quota hinders building homes for the middle class and starters with middle incomes. The VVD wants to build according to the needs of all target groups, with a healthy balance between rent and ownership, to maintain a vital and mixed society.

239952.explanation: Provided it fits in with the living environment and the character of Zeewolde, high-rise buildings are a good option to quickly realize more homes. The current high-rises, for example along the dike, have a beautiful appearance.

239953.explanation: VVD Zeewolde sees Oosterwold as a crucial location to accommodate the large housing demand. By starting early with the development of a full second residential core, we ensure the village’s growth and offer space to various groups in a green environment.

239954.explanation: We trust in the personal responsibility of residents and entrepreneurs. Aesthetic regulations are often subjective and cause delays. The VVD advocates for unrestricted building and renovation throughout Zeewolde to give initiators more freedom and speed up procedures.

239955.explanation: Given the current housing shortage, it is unacceptable that usable homes stand empty or are not optimally utilized. The VVD wants permanent residency in recreational homes possible under clear conditions, striving for tailored solutions for existing situations.

239956.explanation: To realize housing ambitions, it is necessary to also look at the edges of our village. Where infill is no longer sufficient, building on agricultural land on the village borders should be discussed to realize the desperately needed homes for starters and families.

239957.explanation: The G-area is strategically located between the current village and the Polderwijk. The VVD chooses to build homes at this location to directly tackle the housing shortage. A green character for the neighborhood can go hand in hand with necessary housing construction.

239959.explanation: The Aanloophaven is a showcase for Zeewolde. We want to strengthen this area as a recreational attraction and 'lively heart'. This could be done by converting the town hall into housing and allowing hospitality in the ground floor.

239960.explanation: Although housing construction has priority, this must be accompanied by efficient municipal management. The VVD currently sees no need for the costly relocation of the municipal yard but does see eventual housing construction at the current location.

239961.explanation: Current schemes in Zeewolde are carefully designed. The VVD believes that work is the best way out of poverty. Instead of generic relaxation, we focus on a human approach and tailored solutions for those who truly need it, with a strong focus on activation towards work.

239962.explanation: Social security is based on reciprocity. The VVD considers it fair that people receiving benefits contribute in accordance with their capacity by doing socially useful work. This promotes integration, keeps skills sharp, and increases chances of returning to the labor market.

239963.explanation: The VVD chooses sustainable exit to regular work instead of creating subsidized jobs, which often leads to disguised unemployment. We focus on effective reintegration, retraining, and close cooperation with local businesses to help people secure a full-fledged job.

239965.explanation: The realization and operation of Park & Ride facilities with commercial services such as bike sharing is primarily a task for the market, not the government. The municipality can facilitate this by providing spatial frameworks, but should not take the entrepreneur's place.

239966.explanation: Although better access to the polder is essential, the VVD sees more benefit in optimizing existing routes like the N302 and N305. A tunnel or bridge is financially costly; assistance from the Defense Ministry might be possible.

239967.explanation: Zeewolde must remain accessible to everyone. Environmental zones create unnecessary barriers for residents and entrepreneurs without significantly improving local air quality. The VVD prefers to encourage innovation rather than banning vehicles.

239969.explanation: The VVD supports the opening of Lelystad Airport. The airport is vital for regional economic development and employment. Leaving investments (200 million) unused is capital destruction and harmful to the business climate.

239970.explanation: A ban from 7:00 PM is disproportionately strict and hampers profitable airport operations. We advocate for a proper balance between economic interests and livability, in accordance with the already established national noise and nuisance frameworks.

239971.explanation: For the VVD, the focus is not on the size of the budget but on the effectiveness of its use. We want to invest in prevention and early detection to avoid more intensive care pathways. The emphasis is on an 'outreach' approach whereby youth workers are present where young people are.

239972.explanation: The costs of youth care are rising rapidly, while not every support request requires a medical solution. The VVD wants to keep youth care sustainable for those who truly need it by 'normalizing' everyday parenting and growing-up questions. This is achieved by applying stricter criteria and limiting the scope of assistance.

239973.explanation: The VVD advocates for a strong local poverty policy focused on structural support for those on minimum incomes. Providing specific products is fundamentally an individual responsibility. Residents facing financial difficulties are helped through existing income-related arrangements.

239974.explanation: The VVD Zeewolde attaches great value to prevention and a healthy lifestyle. We support education about the dangers of alcohol, drugs, and gambling, preferably in close cooperation with the Public Health Service and schools. We do not impose this on schools but encourage them.

239975.explanation: The VVD Zeewolde believes that the shelter for homeless and roofless people can be organized most effectively and humanely at the regional level. Collaboration in Flevoland provides the necessary specialized care and capacity that a single municipality like Zeewolde cannot optimally manage alone.

239914.explanation: The decision has been made by the council; the council chamber is becoming too small. The town hall currently does not comply with occupational health and safety regulations, so it must be renovated.

239917.explanation: We already have the Zeewolde Social Domain Advisory Council (formerly the citizen's table) and the digital Zeewolde Citizen Panel.

239918.explanation: The municipality depends on this income. If we lower the property tax, cuts will have to be made in other areas.

239919.explanation: There are already schemes in place to assist low-income earners.

239921.explanation: Although we do not support the plan for a meeting forest, we believe a reliable government should not reverse a decision once taken.

239922.explanation: We want to adhere to existing environmental requirements. These requirements must be achievable for businesses.

239923.explanation: PuurZeewolde already fulfills this need. The CDA especially wants to make the center a lively area.

239924.explanation: A new industrial estate has already been purchased. It is still empty due to grid congestion. More industrial estates are not needed in the short term.

239925.explanation: The CDA agrees with this statement regarding hospitality businesses. For retail entrepreneurs, we disagree; there is sufficient space within existing rules.

239928.explanation: The deficits in youth care are already enormous; cutting budgets is irresponsible. Spending must be managed more efficiently.

239929.explanation: According to our constitution, the government is obliged to provide a certain percentage.

239930.explanation: The municipality must support entrepreneurs to ensure their international workers participate well in society.

239931.explanation: Labor migrants lose their housing once their employment contract ends. Entrepreneurs who knowingly put people on the street are responsible for these workers leaving.

239932.explanation: This is a requirement above the legal minimum. The maximum allowable standard is 40%.

239933.explanation: Anyone using a fireplace or wood stove must comply with the burning regulations.

239934.explanation: This fits within the framework of circular Zeewolde. The waste collection point separates and processes waste in a circular manner.

239935.explanation: The G-area was never intended as a nature reserve.

239936.explanation: We should not place solar panels on fertile farmland if there are still rooftops without solar panels.

239937.explanation: We do not want to restrict activity in the agricultural sector by default.

239938.explanation: Being a farmer is a profession.

239940.explanation: We want to maintain the existing scheme.

239941.explanation: Cuts have already been made to art and culture. Further cuts would harm the sector's sustainability.

239942.explanation: Sportpark de Horst is, in our opinion, located appropriately.

239943.explanation: Agreed, provided these are defense-related training programs.

239944.explanation: We would like to support our local entrepreneurs.

239946.explanation: The situation in Zeewolde calls for different resources.

239948.explanation: It has a magnet effect for drug use.

239949.explanation: More camera surveillance should be part of the security pact.

239950.explanation: Residents of Zeewolde who want to continue living in Zeewolde should be given every opportunity to do so.

239951.explanation: We support 40% affordable housing, including social rental homes.

239952.explanation: This way, you can fit more homes into the same space without further disrupting the streetscape.

239953.explanation: The Oosterwold assignment is too big a challenge for the municipality of Zeewolde.

239954.explanation: We find the current advisory committee sufficient.

239955.explanation: Recreational areas are not designated for housing.

239956.explanation: The CDA wants to make a balanced consideration between forest areas and agricultural land where new homes can be built. Our focus is on infill first.

239957.explanation: The decision about the G-area has been made, although it was once intended for housing development.

239959.explanation: The Aanloophaven has sufficient hospitality venues. More focus should be placed on hospitality around the church square.

239960.explanation: The municipal office will be expanded on the site of the municipal yard.

239961.explanation: The existing arrangement is good; relaxation is not necessary.

239962.explanation: We want to encourage people to contribute to society.

239963.explanation: These jobs are a good stepping stone for the next step in a career.

239966.explanation: We want to improve the connections with the mainland.

239967.explanation: The core residential area of Zeewolde is not large enough for an environmental zone.

239969.explanation: It creates more employment in the region and strengthens the economy.

239970.explanation: The focus must be on opening Lelystad Airport. Additional rules can be discussed at a later stage.

239971.explanation: Spending money is not an end in itself; if necessary, we want to spend more on youth and young people’s work. Efforts must become more efficient.

239972.explanation: In our opinion, the problem does not lie here in Zeewolde.

239973.explanation: This is a basic need that must be met.

239974.explanation: We want to apply appropriate measures to combat addictions and promote awareness.

239975.explanation: We already have enough shelter places for those in need of help.

239914.explanation: D66 stands for an accessible and modern municipal office for all residents of Zeewolde. It is the house of democracy.

239917.explanation: A citizen’s council fits within a modern democracy. D66 sees a citizen’s council as a powerful tool in an innovative democracy and for stimulating citizen engagement.

239918.explanation: Property tax (OZB) must remain affordable and realistic. D66 chooses a solid financial policy in the interest of maintenance and preservation of facilities.

239919.explanation: D66 stands for equal opportunities for everyone. Sports and culture should be accessible to all. D66 wants to encourage participation in sports and culture through a village pass.

239921.explanation: According to D66, there is too little support in society for a meeting forest. Additionally, D66 believes that we should use resources and scarce space consciously and responsibly.

239922.explanation: D66 believes companies should take into account the best available techniques regarding environmental requirements.

239923.explanation: It is important to put Zeewolde well on the map. An attractive Zeewolde is in everyone’s interest, especially for entrepreneurs, and it is important to pay attention to promotional campaigns.

239924.explanation: More business parks should not be an end in itself. Future developments will have to show whether more business parks are needed.

239928.explanation: Youth is the future, and we take responsibility regarding youth care.

239929.explanation: D66 believes that the municipality of Zeewolde must comply with the legal obligations. At the same time, the municipality of Zeewolde must build more.

239930.explanation: D66 believes newcomers should be guided in integration so they can participate in society quickly.

239931.explanation: Migrant workers deserve decent housing. The (respective) entrepreneur also has a role in this.

239932.explanation: It is important that the fire station meets its own energy demand. Additionally, the fire station may not block the connections of other entrepreneurs in Zeewolde.

239933.explanation: D66 has already urged the college of mayor and aldermen to sign the Clean Air Agreement. We support cleaner air due to health and climate reasons.

239934.explanation: D66 wants to continue promoting circularity.

239935.explanation: In the G-area, a balance can be found between nature and housing.

239936.explanation: Provided it is well integrated into the landscape.

239937.explanation: In the context of the current profound agricultural transition, large livestock farms are not appropriate.

239938.explanation: D66 believes the municipality may encourage the development of urban agriculture among residents without using scarce land for it. Examples include vertical farming, vegetable gardens, green roofs, etc. This also benefits social cohesion and serves an educational purpose.

239940.explanation: Every child should have the opportunity to play sports and be active.

239941.explanation: Art and culture are the cement of society.

239942.explanation: The current sports complex is too small.

239943.explanation: D66 is a party focused on education and believes we should seize opportunities for a broad educational offer and be able to provide this to our residents.

239944.explanation: It’s about the art. The quality of the artwork is leading.

239946.explanation: The use of force lies primarily with the police.

239948.explanation: This must be investigated first. If it turns out that a coffee shop can contribute to reducing drug-related crime, then D66 will not be opposed. It must be a foundation-run coffee shop without profit motive.

239949.explanation: Where privacy is guaranteed. Cameras increase the feeling of safety.

239950.explanation: D66 believes everyone has the right to equal opportunities.

239951.explanation: Considering the great need for more affordable housing, D66 chooses the following distribution: 45% cheap/social rental, 30% mid-priced owner-occupied, and 25% expensive owner-occupied.

239952.explanation: We live in a housing crisis. Building is not possible or allowed everywhere. Eventually, we must build upwards.

239953.explanation: D66 aims to first build within and at the edges of the existing core. We see sufficient possibilities for this.

239954.explanation: The advantages of aesthetic regulations are preserving and strengthening spatial quality, protecting cultural-historical and urban planning values, and ensuring a harmonious appearance of buildings in an area, which contributes to an attractive living environment.

239955.explanation: Recreational homes are intended for recreation (temporary stay). Moreover, the surroundings of recreational homes are usually not designed for permanent residence, considering that facilities are often too far away.

239956.explanation: In times of housing crisis, one must dare to make difficult choices.

239957.explanation: There is no need for a meeting forest. There is a need for housing. Living and nature can go hand in hand.

239959.explanation: The Aanloophaven is located right next to the village center, within walking distance of shops, and is an ideal location for more hospitality and recreational activities.

239960.explanation: The growth and development of Zeewolde will ultimately require relocation. Choices have already been made regarding the renovation of the municipal yard. So a solution has been found for the coming years.

239961.explanation: Poverty alleviation schemes should be more flexible and accessible because many residents still get into trouble despite existing schemes, partly due to complex legislation and a lack of knowledge about available help.

239962.explanation: No one chooses to receive social assistance voluntarily. People who can (again) work should be helped to do so. Coercion and pressure are not the right solutions.

239963.explanation: People distant from the labor market should be helped and guided in finding a suitable job. Investing money in subsidies is not the solution.

239965.explanation: Good for people and nature. D66 strives for a sustainable society.

239966.explanation: Better and faster connections with Ermelo and Harderwijk are not only good for our entrepreneurs but also solve public transport problems.

239967.explanation: Not everyone (yet) has an electric car. A low-emission zone in Zeewolde is currently not under consideration.

239969.explanation: The decision lies with the national government. Social and economic effects, environment and climate, mobility and accessibility are among the issues under discussion.

239970.explanation: Night restrictions are normal. D66 is open to prohibiting wholesale traffic in the evening and night. The final decision on this is made by the national government.

239971.explanation: The youth of Zeewolde have the future. D66 believes we as a society must continue investing in our children.

239972.explanation: Youth care has been cut for years. Rules should not become stricter (that should not be a goal in itself) but should contribute to youth care.

239973.explanation: Girls and women should not have to worry about the availability of tampons and sanitary pads. They should have easy access to these necessary products, wherever they are.

239974.explanation: Young people are vulnerable. Good education and information can help prevent addiction among youth and should be part of a long-running effective campaign (think of the BOB campaign).

239975.explanation: There must always be a place for homeless and roofless people within our municipality.

239914.explanation: The decision to renovate the town hall was made carefully. Reversing it will lead to extra costs and delays. We choose to carry out the plan sustainably and responsibly so the town hall is future-proof and continues to function well.

239917.explanation: In major decisions, it is important to involve residents early and seriously. A citizens’ assembly strengthens democracy, brings together different perspectives, and helps make better-supported decisions.

239918.explanation: A general reduction of the property tax (ozb) reduces the budget for facilities. We choose a fair distribution of burdens, where those with the broadest shoulders contribute more and the municipality can continue investing in care, housing, and livability.

239919.explanation: Sports and culture must be accessible to everyone. With a discount pass, we ensure that a low income is not an obstacle to participation, because participation contributes to health, social interaction, and equal opportunities.

239921.explanation: The G-area is valuable for nature and tranquility. We believe plans for the Meeting Forest do not fit here and choose to protect the landscape, with recreation in places where nature and quality of life are less pressured.

239922.explanation: The municipality must adopt sustainable procurement as the norm. In tenders, we set clear requirements for the environment and labor conditions, so public money rewards companies that work responsibly and with a future focus.

239923.explanation: Tourism can contribute to the local economy, provided it suits the scale and tranquility of Zeewolde. Targeted promotion can help strengthen recreation, as long as nature, livability, and amenities for residents remain prioritized.

239924.explanation: Zeewolde’s existing industrial areas are largely allocated. GL-PvdA Zeewolde therefore supports the development of Trekkersveld 4 (max. 35 ha) for locally rooted, sustainable companies. This stimulates the economy and employment without unnecessarily cutting into new landscapes.

239925.explanation: Entrepreneurs are given room for customization in opening hours, within clear municipal frameworks. The municipality monitors livability, safety, and peace in the center and intervenes where extensions lead to nuisance.

239928.explanation: Youth care is not a cost item, but an investment in the future. Children and families who need help must not suffer as a result. The municipality must ensure accessible and quality youth care.

239929.explanation: People with a residence permit must be able to participate in society as quickly as possible. Timely housing prevents long-term shelter stays and promotes integration. This also requires extra effort on new construction, so others no longer have to wait.

239930.explanation: Investing in integration pays off. By supporting newcomers with language, work, and participation, we strengthen cohesion in society and prevent problems in the long term.

239931.explanation: No one should end up on the street due to loss of work or housing. The municipality must support labor migrants in finding temporary shelter and guide them towards stable living and working situations, together with employers.

239932.explanation: By making agreements about high energy self-sufficiency, the new barracks can contribute to relieving the electricity grid. This way we use the barracks as an opportunity for local energy solutions and strengthen Zeewolde's energy supply.

239933.explanation: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves remains permitted but must not cause nuisance or health risks. The municipality must focus on education and clear rules to protect air quality and living comfort.

239934.explanation: By developing the waste disposal station into a circular center, we stimulate reuse and repair. This reduces waste, saves raw materials, and offers residents an accessible place to give items a second life.

239935.explanation: The G-area is of great value for nature, tranquility, and landscape. We want to protect and preserve this area as a nature reserve so that biodiversity and quality of life remain guaranteed for future generations.

239936.explanation: Meadows are intended for agriculture and biodiversity protection. Solar panels belong on roofs, parking lots, and business parks. This way we work on sustainable energy without unnecessarily affecting farmland and landscape.

239937.explanation: Large livestock farms put substantial pressure on nature, environment, and living conditions. We choose agriculture that fits the landscape, with attention to animal welfare, health, and a sustainable future for the rural area.

239938.explanation: Urban agriculture connects food production with nature, education, and meeting places. By providing space for urban agriculture, we strengthen local food chains, green the city, and increase residents' involvement.

239940.explanation: Every child must be able to play sports regardless of their parents' income. By reimbursing sports memberships for low-income families, we invest in health, self-confidence, and equal opportunities.

239941.explanation: Art and culture are not a luxury but contribute to encounters, identity, and livability. Even during budget cuts, we want to protect these facilities so culture remains accessible to everyone.

239942.explanation: New sports facilities should be easily accessible and fit carefully into the environment. We prefer strengthening and making existing sports locations more sustainable over expanding at the village edges.

239943.explanation: By urging Defense for MBO and HBO programs that align with the core functions of the barracks (medical, logistics, and IT/communication), we create training opportunities for young people and strengthen the regional labor market.

239944.explanation: By involving local artists more often in cultural activities, we strengthen the cultural life in Zeewolde and give homegrown talent a stage. This keeps culture close, visible, and accessible to residents.

239946.explanation: Borough officers (BOAs) must be able to do their work safely. In situations where necessary, a baton can aid their protection, provided this is accompanied by proper training, clear regulations, and strict proportionality.

239948.explanation: A regulated coffeeshop ensures controlled sales and reduces illegal trade and nuisance. With clear rules and supervision, this can contribute to safety and public health in Zeewolde.

239949.explanation: Camera surveillance is not a structural solution and affects privacy. We primarily choose visible enforcement, good lighting, and social prevention. Cameras can be deployed temporarily and targeted, but only as a last resort.

239950.explanation: Social rental homes must be distributed fairly based on urgency and waiting time. Prioritizing by place of residence is unjust and does not help solve the housing shortage.

239951.explanation: The share of social rental housing is currently about 21%. To tackle the housing shortage, a significant catch-up is needed. By realizing at least 45% social rental in new construction, we ensure a balanced and affordable housing stock.

239952.explanation: More height can help address the housing shortage without cutting extra open space. Flats up to 6 floors are possible if they are carefully integrated and contribute to affordable homes and a pleasant living environment.

239953.explanation: A second residential core in Oosterwold can help accommodate housing demand, provided it is carefully and phased developed from 2030 onwards with attention to accessibility, amenities, nature, and energy.

239954.explanation: Aesthetic guidelines ensure coherence and quality in the built environment. Instead of abolishing them, we want to make rules simpler and clearer so building remains possible without deteriorating the streetscape and living environment.

239955.explanation: Holiday homes are not intended for permanent residence and often lack appropriate facilities. By not allowing this, we protect livability, safety, and a fair housing market. The solution lies in sufficient regular and affordable housing.

239956.explanation: To address the housing shortage, new construction on farmland may be necessary. This must be done carefully and phased, preserving landscape and nature, with clear choices for affordable housing and good facilities.

239957.explanation: The G-area is intended as a nature and recreation area. Housing does not fit here and would come at the expense of landscape, tranquility, and biodiversity. We choose to protect this area and realize homes in more suitable locations.

239959.explanation: The Aanloophaven offers opportunities for recreation and meeting places. By further developing this area, we strengthen Zeewolde's attractiveness, with attention to nature, livability, and a good balance between recreation and tranquility.

239960.explanation: The location of the municipal yard is well suited for housing. By building apartments here and relocating the yard to Schepenveld, we make better use of space and create additional homes in a logical place.

239961.explanation: Poverty assistance must be accessible to everyone who needs it. By simplifying schemes and actively promoting them, we ensure more people receive timely support.

239962.explanation: People on social assistance often face major personal challenges. We believe support and guidance should be central, not obligations. Voluntary participation works better than coercion.

239963.explanation: Subsidized jobs give people who have been unemployed for a long time a renewed perspective on work and income. This prevents long-term dependence on benefits and invests in talent, confidence, and social participation.

239965.explanation: Park & Ride spots on the outskirts of Zeewolde reduce the number of cars in the village. With shared bikes and charging stations, we make sustainable travel easy and keep the center safe and livable.

239966.explanation: A new bridge or tunnel between Zeewolde and Ermelo/Harderwijk has a major impact on nature and landscape. We believe existing infrastructure should be better utilized and improved first before considering such drastic and costly projects.

239967.explanation: Clean air and health are important. We do not exclude an environmental zone upfront but believe measures must be effective and fair. We choose customization and assess per situation what truly contributes to a healthier living environment.

239969.explanation: Holiday flights from Lelystad Airport burden Zeewolde's living environment with extra noise, emissions, and traffic pressure. The municipality must stand up for the health, nature, and quality of life of residents. Therefore, we oppose commercial flights.

239970.explanation: Night rest is essential for health and well-being. By prohibiting flights between 7:00 PM and 7:00 AM, we protect residents from noise nuisance and sleep disturbance.

239971.explanation: Youth work prevents problems and offers perspective. In Zeewolde, the demand for support is growing. GL-PvdA wants to invest in accessible youth care. Stricter rules make help less effective and increase problems.

239972.explanation: Children and families must receive help in time if needed. Stricter rules raise barriers and can worsen problems. We choose accessible and appropriate youth care.

239973.explanation: Menstrual products are not a luxury. By making them available for free at schools and community centers, we combat period poverty and ensure everyone can participate fully.

239974.explanation: Prevention is more effective than intervention afterwards. Together with schools, the municipality can better inform and support young people to prevent addiction.

239975.explanation: Everyone has the right to a safe place to live. By providing sufficient shelter, we prevent distressing situations and offer people hope for recovery.

239914.explanation: Spending so much money on own housing is actually inappropriate given the long waiting lists for affordable rental homes.

239917.explanation: Unnecessary, because there are already sufficient opportunities for participation, including through citizen council members, public speaking opportunities, and roundtable meetings.

239918.explanation: Due to the increase in property value assessments (WOZ) and the growth in the number of properties in Zeewolde, a reduction of the property tax (OZB) is within reach.

239919.explanation: A discount pass for sports and culture removes financial barriers, encourages participation, and prevents social isolation. It strengthens health, self-confidence, and social cohesion, so everyone in Zeewolde can participate.

239921.explanation: The costs are exorbitantly high and the area already offers significant natural value in its current form.

239922.explanation: A justified demand: Zeewolde is growing, but not at the expense of nature and health. Strict environmental standards protect residents, limit damage, and ensure only future-proof businesses settle here.

239923.explanation: More tourists and day-trippers strengthen our local economy and entrepreneurs. With a targeted campaign together with Puur Zeewolde, Zeewolde will gain better recognition and we will make the most of our recreational opportunities.

239924.explanation: Zeewolde must speed up the development of Trekkersveld 4.

239925.explanation: Yes, provided that entrepreneurs have the freedom to determine their own opening hours without unnecessarily patronizing rules. However, those who want to stay open at night also bear responsibility for peace, safety, and public order.

239928.explanation: Cutting youth care is short-sighted. Timely support prevents more serious problems later on. Children and families must be able to rely on help, not be put on waiting lists.

239929.explanation: Social housing is scarce. Priority for status holders is unfair to residents who have been waiting for years. Integration support is necessary, but without pushing others further back.

239930.explanation: Municipal funds are primarily for our own residents: youth care, health care, and housing shortages. Integration is important, but must not mean that Zeewolde residents are put last.

239931.explanation: Housing is already scarce. The municipality must first help its own residents and tackle abuses by employers and landlords. Labor migration must not put extra pressure on social housing.

239932.explanation: A barracks that is largely energy-neutral reduces costs, makes us less dependent, and fits Zeewolde's sustainable ambitions. Moreover, it strengthens safety and supply security.

239933.explanation: Open fireplaces and wood stoves can remain, but used sensibly. Burn clean wood, prevent nuisance, and do not burn during calm or foggy weather. Freedom and responsibility.

239934.explanation: A repair workshop is especially valuable: items get a second life, it saves costs and waste, and it helps residents who prefer repairing over throwing away.

239935.explanation: Turning the G-area into a Meeting Forest costs millions better spent on youth care, health care, and housing shortages.

239936.explanation: Cows and sheep belong to graze on meadowlands!

239937.explanation: You can't just forbid that: permits and property rights matter, and there is national legislation. The municipality can steer strictly through spatial rules and environmental requirements, instead of a general ban.

239938.explanation: Good idea: urban agriculture makes Zeewolde greener and healthier, strengthens local food supply, and brings residents together. It uses vacant areas smartly and contributes to education and biodiversity.

239940.explanation: That scheme already exists! To prevent children from becoming isolated, Zeewolde invests via the Youth Fund Sports & Culture in health and involvement.

239941.explanation: If cuts must be made, every spending item must be open for discussion — including art and culture subsidies. We must prioritize core tasks such as safety, care, and youth care. Culture remains valuable but is not automatically untouchable.

239942.explanation: A new sports complex on the village outskirts is needed to accommodate growth and sports demand. This creates future-proof sports facilities, better accessibility, and sufficient space, aligned with the plans around sport complex Horst.

239943.explanation: That is up to the Defense Ministry itself.

239944.explanation: Give priority to artists from Zeewolde so local talent and creativity can also be visible in our own public space. That strengthens our identity, pride, and involvement with the village.

239946.explanation: Enforcement officers increasingly work in unsafe situations. A baton may be necessary for self-protection and to act immediately, but only with proper training, clear rules, and strict oversight.

239948.explanation: A coffeeshop can cause extra nuisance, increase enforcement pressure, and have a magnet effect. Zeewolde already faces enough challenges; the municipality must prioritize safety, youth, and livability.

239949.explanation: Camera surveillance increases the chances of apprehension and acts preventively. Effective video monitoring is needed not only in the center but also in rural areas to better tackle theft, vandalism, and illegal dumping.

239950.explanation: Zeewolde Liberaal wants to quickly eliminate waiting lists. Therefore, we choose priority for starters, young families, and elderly residents moving within Zeewolde, so residents regain perspective on affordable housing in their own village.

239951.explanation: With the enormous waiting lists, new construction must include at least 45% social housing. Only in this way can we give starters, families, and people with low or middle incomes a fair chance for affordable housing in Zeewolde.

239952.explanation: With smart high-rise buildings up to 6 floors, we can build more homes within the village without sacrificing additional farmland or nature areas. This helps against the housing shortage and keeps Zeewolde compact and livable.

239953.explanation: A second residential center in Oosterwold offers opportunities to alleviate housing shortages for young people and young families and to stimulate housing flow. But it is not a replacement: at Zeewolde Liberaal, it's both building within the village and in Oosterwold.

239954.explanation: No additional aesthetic regulations. There are already enough rules for building and renovating. Less patronizing means more freedom for residents and entrepreneurs, less delay, and lower costs, as long as safety and quality are guaranteed.

239955.explanation: Allowing recreational homes as permanent residences can help alleviate housing shortages for young people and families. With clear rules, we prevent abuse and improve livability.

239956.explanation: Agricultural and forest land in Flevoland is extremely fertile and provides food, nature, CO₂ storage, and water retention. Building means irreversible loss and extra costs. Zeewolde Liberaal opts for infill, vacant sites, and relocation of De Horst.

239957.explanation: Building in the G-area is not necessary. If sports park De Horst moves to the village edge, space will be freed up for housing in a location where that is possible. This way we preserve the Meeting Forest and still build homes.

239959.explanation: Further development of the Aanloophaven into a recreational area with hospitality makes Zeewolde more attractive for residents and visitors. It strengthens the local economy, increases vibrancy, and better utilizes our location by the water.

239960.explanation: This is a smart trade. The municipal yard in that location no longer makes sense; apartments can be built there instead, close to amenities. Moving it to Schepenveld creates space, reduces nuisance, and improves accessibility.

239961.explanation: Regulations need to be more flexible. Bureaucracy must never be an obstacle for residents who are structurally in financial trouble through no fault of their own. Help should be quick, humane, and accessible, without unnecessary barriers.

239962.explanation: Socially meaningful work provides structure and rhythm, helps build work experience, and increases the chance of transitioning to regular employment. This way we promote independence and participation instead of being left on the sidelines.

239963.explanation: There must be more subsidized jobs, including within the municipality itself. With job carving, tasks can be redistributed smartly to create suitable positions. Not only businesses but also the government must take responsibility here.

239965.explanation: Park & Ride with rental bikes sounds good, but in practice it is often expensive and vulnerable. It leads to theft and misuse and often ends as a costly failure—like the ambitious GO Sharing recently. Zeewolde must invest in solutions that actually work and remain affordable.

239966.explanation: A tunnel or bridge towards Ermelo/Harderwijk places extra traffic pressure exactly on Zeewolde-South. The Vlinder and Vissen neighborhoods already suffer from the A28 and will soon also be impacted by the coastal development near Horst and Nulde. That is irresponsible.

239967.explanation: No environmental zone in Zeewolde. We don’t have a large city with structural smog problems, but we do have residents and entrepreneurs who depend on their car or bus. An environmental zone mainly adds rules, costs, and exclusion without demonstrable effect. Choose smart traffic flow and cleaner mobility.

239969.explanation: Holiday flights from Lelystad Airport are a national government decision. The cabinet decides and the House of Representatives ratifies. Municipal “resistance” is mainly symbolic and a waste of time. Those who want to stop this must do so at the national level.

239970.explanation: This is part of the Lelystad Airport airport decision and is therefore a national authority. The municipality cannot enforce this; “insisting” is mainly symbolic. The real discussion and decision-making lie with the cabinet and House of Representatives.

239971.explanation: More money doesn’t automatically mean better results. Zeewolde must first work more efficiently and stop hiring expensive advisors from outside Flevoland. Focus on local expertise and targeted, measurable assistance—not on ever-increasing budgets without clear effect.

239972.explanation: The rules must be stricter. The influx into youth care must be curbed so that help remains available for those who really need it. At the same time, we invest heavily in prevention and early support to reduce the escalation of problems.

239973.explanation: This is primarily a private expense and not a core task of the municipality. If we spend money here, we would have to reimburse all kinds of other daily costs as well. Targeted assistance through minimum arrangements or food banks is better for those who really need it.

239974.explanation: Prevention is better than cure. Together with schools, the municipality can reach young people early with honest information and resilience training so that problematic use and addiction have less chance. This prevents much misery, care costs, and school dropout later on.

239975.explanation: Homelessness is increasing, which calls for quick, concrete action. More shelter places provide people safety and peace and enable guidance towards care, work, and permanent housing. This prevents further escalation of problems on the street.

statements.title: The G area should become a nature reserve.
Housing applicants from Zeewolde should be given priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: Woningzoekenden uit Zeewolde moeten voorrang krijgen bij een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: The municipality should make poverty assistance programs more flexible.
The municipality should start a campaign together with schools to combat addiction among young people.
Zeewolde should allow high-rise buildings of six storeys.

titleParts.glossary: Buildings with homes or businesses on multiple floors, for example apartments or flats.

statements.title: The municipality should primarily engage artists from Zeewolde for public art.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vooral kunstenaars uit Zeewolde inschakelen voor kunst in de

titleParts.glossary: Outdoor areas that are accessible to everyone, such as streets, squares, and parks.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality must not cut spending on youth care.
There should be more CCTV surveillance in Zeewolde.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Park & Ride sites should be created on the outskirts of Zeewolde with shared bikes and charging stations.
A new sports complex should be built on the edge of the village.
At least 45% of new-build housing should be social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Bij nieuwbouw moet minstens 45% bestaan uit

titleParts.glossary: Rental homes with low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: Cafés, restaurants and shops should be able to set their own opening hours.
The municipality should help migrant workers who lose their housing to find another place to live.

titleParts.glossary: People from another country who work temporarily in the Netherlands.

titleParts.text: die hun woning verliezen helpen om een andere woonplek te vinden.

statements.title: Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) should be equipped with a baton.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

statements.title: No large livestock farms should be allowed in the rural area.

titleParts.text: Er mogen geen grote veestallen in het

titleParts.glossary: Area within the municipality's borders but outside the towns or villages themselves; there are few houses and other buildings here.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in a promotional campaign to attract more tourists and day-trippers to Zeewolde.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in een

titleParts.glossary: A series of advertisements on, for example, billboards, television, and social media.

titleParts.text: om meer toeristen en dagjesmensen naar Zeewolde te trekken.

statements.title: The municipality should introduce a discount card that gives low-income people discounts on sports and cultural activities.
The municipality should urge the provinces to build a tunnel or bridge connecting Zeewolde with Ermelo and Harderwijk.
The municipality should pay sports club membership fees for children from low-income families.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Zeewolde should have a citizens' assembly that advises the municipal council. A citizens' assembly consists of residents selected by lot.
The municipality should allow solar panels on pastureland.
The municipality should allow a coffeeshop in Zeewolde.

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Apartments should be built on the municipal depot site. The municipal depot itself should be relocated to the Schepenveld.
The municipality should distribute free tampons and sanitary pads at schools and community centers.
The municipality should press the national government to ensure the new barracks supply 80% of their own energy.
The municipality should give status holders priority in the allocation of social housing.

titleParts.glossary: Refugees who have been granted a residence permit and are therefore allowed to stay in the Netherlands.

titleParts.text: voorrang geven bij de toewijzing van een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: The Aanloophaven should be further developed into a recreational area with restaurants and cafés.
The municipality should oppose holiday flights from Lelystad Airport.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should impose strict environmental requirements on companies that want to establish themselves in Zeewolde.
The municipality should press the Ministry of Defence to set up vocational (MBO) and higher professional (HBO) education on the barracks site.
The municipality should not introduce aesthetic rules for construction and renovation.
The municipality should spend more money to help newcomers integrate into society.
The municipality should allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residential address.
The decision to renovate the town hall must be reversed.
The municipality should spend more money on youth work and services.
Municipal budget cuts may come at the expense of subsidies for art and culture.
The waste drop-off site should become a circular center with a recycling area and a repair workshop.
Zeewolde should reverse the decision to create the Ontmoetingsbos in the G-area.
There should be more subsidized jobs for people who face barriers to employment.
More business parks should be developed in Zeewolde.
By 2030 there should be a second residential center in Oosterwold.
The municipality should continue to allow the use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves.
The municipality should allow new construction on agricultural land.
Instead of the planned "Meeting Forest," housing should be built in the G-area.
The municipality should urge the national government to ban flights at Lelystad Airport between 7:00 PM and 7:00 AM after it opens.
The rules for receiving youth care should be made stricter.
The municipality should ensure there is more urban agriculture.
There should be no low-emission zone in Zeewolde.
The municipality should provide more shelter places for homeless people.
